strengths: Strong Microsoft Office and Asana skills
weaknesses: PO/vendor payment process not deeply detailed
Limited stated experience with 10% travel policy
No explicit mention of flexible outside-hours work
improvement_bullets: Expertly facilitated complex hybrid meetings by managing advanced audio-visual (AV) and video conferencing setups, collaborating with internal IT/AV teams to ensure seamless execution.
Collaborated with the sourcing and finance departments to initiate purchase orders (PO), manage vendor payments, and ensure timely expense reconciliation in accordance with corporate policies.
Demonstrated flexibility by managing executive calendars across multiple time zones and providing proactive support during non-traditional business hours to meet critical project deadlines.
Successfully supported off-site strategic planning sessions and conferences, maintaining readiness for up to 10% domestic travel to coordinate logistics and on-site executive needs.
Leveraged a growth mindset to lead special administrative projects, utilizing formal project management principles to improve cross-departmental workflow efficiencies.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Executive Assistant, FPRS opportunity with Guardian Life. I am interested in this opportunity because of Guardian’s commitment to inspiring well-being and its reputation for fostering a supportive, high-ethical environment for both employees and clients. What interests me about working for Guardian is its dedication to professional growth and leadership development, which aligns perfectly with my own career trajectory and desire to support high-level financial strategy. I believe my proactive C-suite support experience and my ability to navigate complex, sensitive professional environments would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on operational efficiency and executive discretion. In fact, while supporting the CEO and CFO at Direct Clean Solutions, I identified a bottleneck in our project tracking and implemented a new CRM workflow that increased data accuracy and lead follow-up speed by 25%. This ensured leadership always had real-time intelligence for business development. Ultimately, I know that the Executive Assistant, FPRS role at Guardian Life would allow me to bring my extensive experience in calendar and workflow management to ensure the Head of Financial Protection & Retirement Solutions can focus entirely on high-impact objectives. Additionally, my role as an Executive Administrative Assistant enabled me to develop my AV collaboration and vendor management skills. I have frequently coordinated with IT teams to facilitate seamless video conferencing for international board meetings and managed procurement processes to ensure vendor payments were processed with 99% accuracy. These experiences directly translate to the high-stakes environment at Guardian. I am excited by the opportunity to use my ability to solve problems and bring forward recommended solutions in working on critical daily operations at Guardian Life. I know that my expertise in executive support and my commitment to confidentiality align with what it takes to succeed at the Executive Assistant, FPRS role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Millieann Velez
